---
title: "What Confucius practiced is good for your mind: Examining the effect of a contemplative practice in Confucian tradition on executive functions"
lang: en
language: ../../assets/language-en.yml
toc-title: "On this page"
---

## Method

A pretest-posttest controlled study comparing BMAA, Chan meditation, and a waitlist control group. Both active interventions included 12 hours of practice over four weeks.

## Key Findings

- BMAA produced the clearest improvement in working-memory capacity compared with Chan meditation and waitlist control.
- Chan meditation mainly improved attention switching, while neither active intervention improved Stroop inhibition.

## Reference

Teng, S.-C., & Lien, Y.-W. (2016). What Confucius practiced is good for your mind: Examining the effect of a contemplative practice in Confucian tradition on executive functions. *Consciousness and Cognition, 42*, 204-215. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.concog.2016.03.016
